title: Backtick operator used for shell execution
description: The backtick operator executes its contents as a shell command. This is equivalent to shell_exec() and poses the same command injection risks.
explanation: Backticks execute shell commands. If any part of the command includes user input, it enables OS command injection. Even without user input, shell execution in modules is discouraged.
fix: Remove backtick usage. If shell interaction is truly needed, use escapeshellarg() on all arguments and document why shell access is required.
